question: Adversarially review the proposed identification implementation. Assume the goal is to catch conceptual mistakes before any live rollout. Critique the spec as sharply as possible. Look for: category confusion between identification and other layers; bad analogies to the human body; risks in seeding `identification:working-body`; ways the widening rule could drift, bloat, or collapse into self-referentiality; places where the algorithm is still circular or underspecified; runtime/prompt integration risks; and any reasons this should not be implemented yet. If the design is partly right, state exactly what should be kept versus changed.
notes: This is an adversarial pre-implementation review, not a prompt to be agreeable.
Prefer high-signal objections over broad summaries.
Focus on the first implementable version, not an idealized future system.
